provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'There are many types of communication.'

items.d: when people talk, write and share with people
items.s: There are many kinds of ___, like text messages and group chats.
items.hintDe: Wenn Menschen miteinander reden und schreiben.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'She showed her disappointment.'

items.d: how sad you are when a thing is not good
items.s: She showed her ___ when there were no tickets.
items.hintDe: Das traurige Gefühl, wenn etwas nicht so gut ist wie erhofft.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'There's no excuse.' (cf. SB p.55 msg B 'No excuses, please.')

items.d: a reason you give for a bad thing you did
items.s: There's no ___. You did it!
items.hintDe: Ein Grund, den man für etwas Schlechtes erfindet.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'My friend invited me to a fancy dress party.' (cf. SB p.59 Ex.13B)

items.d: where all the people come in a costume
items.s: My friend invited me to a ___, so I need a costume.
items.hintDe: Eine Party, zu der alle verkleidet kommen.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'How do you say this in German?'

items.d: what people speak in Austria, not English
items.s: In Austria, many people speak ___.
items.hintDe: Die Sprache, die man in Österreich spricht.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'My friends and I have a group chat.'

items.d: lots of friends write here on a mobile phone
items.s: My friends and I have a ___ on our mobile phones.
items.hintDe: Wo viele Freunde gemeinsam am Handy schreiben.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'Honestly, that was only a joke!'

items.d: when you tell the truth and do not lie
items.s: ___, that was a joke!
items.hintDe: Wenn du etwas wirklich ehrlich meinst.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'I'm going to do nothing instead.'

items.d: in place of one thing
items.s: I don't want to do my homework. I'm going to do nothing ___.
items.hintDe: Du machst etwas anderes anstelle der ersten Sache.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'Can we have tickets for row 12, please?' (cf. SB p.61 'row 12, please.')

items.d: the chairs that are all next to your chair at the cinema
items.s: Can we have tickets for ___ 12, please?
items.hintDe: Die Stühle nebeneinander im Kino, zum Beispiel Reihe 12.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'I'm writing a social media post.'

items.d: where people share pictures and short posts with a lot of people
items.s: I'm writing a ___ post.
items.hintDe: Wo Menschen Bilder und Beiträge mit vielen teilen.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'The concert is sold out.' (cf. SB p.60 'It's sold out.')

items.d: when there are no more tickets
items.s: The concert is ___. There are no more tickets.
items.hintDe: Wenn alle Karten schon weg sind.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'There's only one ticket for the 5 o'clock showing. That's a pity!' (cf. SB p.61)

items.d: you tell a friend this when you are sorry about a thing
items.s: There is just one ticket for the 5 o'clock showing. ___
items.hintDe: Das sagst du, wenn dir etwas leidtut.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'Can I have two tickets, please?' (cf. SB p.61)

items.d: you show this to go into the cinema or a concert
items.s: Can I have two ___ for the cinema, please?
items.hintDe: Die Karte, mit der du ins Kino kommst.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'I was very ashamed.'

items.d: to feel very bad about a bad thing you did
items.s: I did a bad thing, so I was very ___.
items.hintDe: Sich schlecht fühlen, weil man etwas Schlechtes getan hat.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'William is always worried.' (cf. SB p.56 'He is worried.')

items.d: to think a bad thing might happen
items.s: William is always ___.
items.hintDe: Denken, dass etwas Schlimmes passieren könnte.

distractorPool: to stay at a friend's house

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'She isn't going to come over today.'

items.d: to visit a friend at their place
items.s: She isn't going to ___ today because she is ill.
items.hintDe: Jemanden zu Hause besuchen.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'Be careful or you might crash.' (cf. SB p.56 'I might crash.')

items.d: to drive into a thing and have an accident
items.s: Be careful or you might ___.
items.hintDe: Mit dem Fahrrad oder Auto gegen etwas fahren.

provenance.sbRef: WB p.54 Ex.3, Aishe: 'I'm going to do nothing at home tonight.'

items.d: to not do anything at all, just take a rest
items.s: I don't want to work. I'm going to ___ this afternoon.
items.hintDe: Gar nichts machen, einfach nur faulenzen.

provenance.sbRef: WB p.53 Ex.1: 'Mehmet is going to do the shopping.'

items.d: to go to the supermarket and buy what you need
items.s: My mum is going to ___ at the supermarket.
items.hintDe: Im Supermarkt einkaufen, was man braucht.

provenance.sbRef: WB p.54 Ex.3, Dad: 'first you have to do your homework.'

items.d: to do the school work your teacher gives you
items.s: First you have to ___, then you can watch TV.
items.hintDe: Die Aufgaben für die Schule machen.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.57 Ex.11 sentence half f: 'I might get into trouble.'

items.d: to do a bad thing and make the teacher angry
items.s: If you don't listen to the teacher, you might ___.
items.hintDe: Etwas Schlechtes tun und dafür Ärger bekommen.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.59 Ex.13A: 'I'm going to have a party on Saturday at my place.'

items.d: to ask friends to come for cake and fun
items.s: It's my birthday on Saturday, so I'm going to ___.
items.hintDe: Freunde zum Feiern einladen.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.55 Ex.4, 'Are you going to play basketball?'

items.d: you run and jump and do this fast with friends
items.s: I'm going to ___ with my friends after school.
items.hintDe: Ein Spiel mit einem großen Ball und einem hohen Netz.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.54 Ex.1 word box: 'stay at a friend's house'

items.w: to stay at a friend's house
items.d: to visit a boy or girl you like and sleep at their place for one night
items.s: Can I ___ on Friday night?

sAnswers.text: to stay at a friend's house

dAnswers.text: to stay at a friend's house

deToEn.text: to stay at a friend's house

items.hintDe: Über Nacht bei einem Freund bleiben.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'I'm going to take it easy this afternoon.'

items.d: to do nothing and be calm
items.s: I'm very tired. I'm going to ___ this afternoon.
items.hintDe: Sich ausruhen und nichts Anstrengendes tun.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.55 msg H: 'Sorry for telling you a lie.'

items.d: to say a thing that is not true
items.s: Always tell the truth. Don't ___.
items.hintDe: Etwas sagen, das nicht wahr ist.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.55 Ex.5 chant: 'I am going to tidy my room.'

items.d: to make a mess clean and put your clothes away
items.s: Mum is angry. I must ___ now because my clothes are all over the bed.
items.hintDe: Dein Zimmer sauber und ordentlich machen.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.55 Ex.4, 'I'm going to watch films.'

items.d: to look at a story on a big screen at the cinema
items.s: We are going to ___ on the big screen at the cinema.
items.hintDe: Sich eine Geschichte auf einer großen Leinwand ansehen.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'I didn't tell her the truth.'

items.d: what is true and not a lie
items.s: I didn't tell her the ___.
items.hintDe: Das, was wirklich wahr ist.

provenance.sbRef: Masterlist exampleSb: 'The concert is sold out. What a shame!' (cf. SB p.60 'What a shame.')

items.d: you tell a friend this when a bad thing happens
items.s: The concert is sold out. ___
items.hintDe: Das sagst du, wenn etwas Schlechtes passiert.
